note: CKEditor 5 Premium Features is the base module for CKEditor's commercial premium add-ons: it holds the license key + Cloud Services auth, exposes two permissions and a config object, and ships ~20 thin feature submodules. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source for; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: I want to use CKEditor 5 Premium Features (track changes, comments, export to PDF) on a Drupal 11 site. What commercial prerequisite do I need, and where do I enter it?
evals.expected_output: A CKEditor commercial license key and/or CKEditor Cloud Services subscription (free trial available). Enter it in the ckeditor5_premium_features.settings config via the settings form at /admin/config/ckeditor5-premium-features/settings (license_key key).

evals.prompt: How is CKEditor 5 Premium Features structured — is enabling the main module enough to get export-to-PDF, comments, and real-time collaboration?
evals.expected_output: No. The main module only provides shared config + Cloud Services authentication. Each premium feature is a separate submodule (e.g. ckeditor5_premium_features_export_pdf, _collaboration, _realtime_collaboration) that you enable individually, then add its button to a text format's CKEditor 5 toolbar.

evals.prompt: How do I turn on export-to-PDF in the CKEditor 5 editor for a specific text format on my Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: Enable the ckeditor5_premium_features_export_pdf submodule (drush en), then edit the text format at /admin/config/content/formats and add the export-to-PDF button to that format's CKEditor 5 toolbar.

evals.prompt: What extra configuration does real-time collaboration require compared to track changes or comments in CKEditor 5 Premium Features?
evals.expected_output: Real-time collaboration requires configuring CKEditor Cloud Services authorization (auth_type = key with Environment ID + Access key, or a dev_token URL). Track changes and Comments can run without real-time collaboration.

evals.prompt: Which config object stores the CKEditor 5 Premium Features license key and Cloud Services credentials, and which key holds the license?
evals.expected_output: The ckeditor5_premium_features.settings config object; the license_key key holds the license, plus auth_type/env/access_key for Cloud Services.

evals.prompt: Which permission should I grant editor roles so they can use the Word/PDF export plugins in CKEditor 5 Premium Features, and which one covers the access token for cloud/real-time features?
evals.expected_output: 'use ckeditor5 exporters endpoints' gates the Word/PDF export plugins; 'use ckeditor5 access token' gates the JWT token endpoint used by cloud services / real-time collaboration.

evals.prompt: I want the AI Assistant feature of CKEditor 5 Premium Features backed by OpenAI. Which submodule and which PHP library (Composer suggest) are involved?
evals.expected_output: Enable the AI submodules (ckeditor5_premium_features_ai / ckeditor5_premium_features_ai_assistant); the openai-php/client library (Composer suggest) is required for the OpenAI/AzureAI provider (aws/aws-sdk-php for AWS Bedrock).

evals.prompt: Which core Drupal modules does CKEditor 5 Premium Features depend on?
evals.expected_output: The core editor and ckeditor5 modules (dependencies: drupal:editor, drupal:ckeditor5 in the info.yml).
